Name: low_latency_inference
Title: Low-Latency Inference with Strict Bounds on Overhead and Spectral Stability
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that by leveraging PAC-Bayes frameworks and spectral stability bounds, we can derive enforceable protocols for latency-critical inference with strict bounds on overhead, thereby improving the efficiency and reliability of machine learning models in real-time applications.
Related Work: Recent works such as SNIP, HiLoRA, EMQ, and AMP-ViT have focused on efficient training and inference methods for large language models and vision transformers. However, these works do not explicitly address the need for strict bounds on overhead and spectral stability in low-latency inference. Our proposal distinguishes itself by prioritizing these aspects, leveraging PAC-Bayes frameworks and spectral stability bounds to derive enforceable protocols for latency-critical inference.
Abstract: We propose a novel framework for low-latency inference with strict bounds on overhead and spectral stability. By leveraging PAC-Bayes frameworks and spectral stability bounds, we derive enforceable protocols for latency-critical inference, ensuring reliable and efficient performance in real-time applications. Our approach prioritizes compute-frugal execution, utilizing sequential or single-pass adaptive thresholds while avoiding parallel sampling to eliminate memory pressure. We evaluate our framework using first-class latency benchmarks with explicit perturbation-cost and memory-bandwidth accounting, targeting throughput-accuracy Pareto frontiers.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Evaluate the effectiveness of our framework on a range of benchmark datasets, including ImageNet and CIFAR-10, using various machine learning models such as ResNet and MobileNet.
Experiment 2: Compare the performance of our framework with state-of-the-art methods, including SNIP, HiLoRA, EMQ, and AMP-ViT, in terms of latency, accuracy, and overhead.

Experiment 3: Investigate the impact of different spectral stability bounds and PAC-Bayes frameworks on the performance of our framework, using techniques such as cross-validation and ablation studies.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of deriving enforceable protocols for latency-critical inference with strict bounds on overhead and spectral stability may be high, requiring significant computational resources and expertise.
The framework may not be applicable to all types of machine learning models or applications, and may require modifications or extensions to accommodate specific use cases.

The evaluation metrics used to assess the performance of our framework may not capture all relevant aspects of low-latency inference, and may require additional metrics or evaluation protocols to ensure comprehensive assessment.
